sora_prompt:
Realistic phone-footage look; clear causal beats; grounded tone. Pacing: medium.
Title: Phone-footage meet-cute with preserved dialogue
Coherence mode: hybrid. Prompt density: balanced.
Brief: A realistic phone-footage style scene: two strangers accidentally swap identical tote bags at a rainy bus stop. They realize it immediately and laugh. Keep it grounded and causal, with a few clear beats. Preserve the dialogue exactly.

Dialogue:
ALEX: "Wait—this is yours. We have the same bag."
MAYA: "No way. Okay, you’re definitely getting the one with the leaky umbrella."
Continuity anchors:
- Identity anchors: keep faces, hair, body type consistent across cuts.
- Wardrobe/props: preserve key items unless explicitly changed.
- Dialogue continuity: each line must be spoken by the correct visible speaker mouth.

Speaker binding (strict): stage dialogue so only one on-screen mouth can plausibly be speaking; avoid cuts during spoken lines; keep cinematic medium/medium-close coverage.

Title: Two-person dialogue attribution stress test
Coherence mode: hybrid. Prompt density: balanced.
Brief: Two friends sit at a kitchen table. One (Jules) is chopping vegetables; the other (Rina) is pouring tea. Keep the framing cinematic medium/medium-close during dialogue and avoid fast cuts. Preserve dialogue exactly.

Dialogue:
JULES: "If I chop any slower, we’ll eat tomorrow."
RINA: "Good. Tomorrow me is hungrier."
Continuity anchors:
- Identity anchors: keep faces, hair, body type consistent across cuts.
- Wardrobe/props: preserve key items unless explicitly changed.
- Dialogue continuity: each line must be spoken by the correct visible speaker mouth.
